abs.description: The Absolute function returns the absolute value of its input: abs(X)

add.description: The Add function adds two same type values: A+B

unique_filter_output.description: The Add function adds two same type values: A+B

and.description: The And function returns true if the two boolean entries values are true: A&B

atan2.description:
The Arc tangent 2 function returns the angle in radians between the 2D vector Vector and the horizontal. (No need to switch x and y as in the usual atan2 function.)
It is the reciprocal of the Cartesian function

unique_filter_output.description:
The Arc tangent 2 function returns the angle in radians between the 2D vector Vector and the horizontal. (No need to switch x and y as in the usual atan2 function.)
It is the reciprocal of the Cartesian function

cartesian.description:
The Cartesian function converts polar coordinates to Cartesian coordinates. (Polar coordinates are a distance from the origin and an angle in radians from the horizontal.)
It is the reciprocal of the Arc tangent 2 function: Length × Float2(cos(Angle), sin(Angle))

unique_filter_output.description:
The Cartesian function converts polar coordinates to Cartesian coordinates. (Polar coordinates are a distance from the origin and an angle in radians from the horizontal.)
It is the reciprocal of the Arc tangent 2 function: Length × Float2(cos(Angle), sin(Angle))

passthrough.description:
The Dot node lets you reroute connections between nodes. Rerouted connections can be hidden by using Dot nodes as portals.

Note: this node is a layout tool only and has no impact on the result of the graph.

The Dot node lets you reroute connections between nodes. Rerouted connections can be hidden by using Dot nodes as portals.

while.description:
The While loop node will execute the Init branch once, then iterate over the Exit cond. and Loop body branches until the Exit cond. branch returns true
Nodes connected to the Exit cond. and Loop body branches cannot be connected to other branches of the graph.
Loops have an implicit maximum number of iterations which can be disabled by setting it to -1.

unique_filter_output.description:
The While loop node will execute the Init branch once, then iterate over the Exit cond. and Loop body branches until the Exit cond. branch returns true
Nodes connected to the Exit cond. and Loop body branches cannot be connected to other branches of the graph.
Loops have an implicit maximum number of iterations which can be disabled by setting it to -1.

bitmap.description: Loads a bitmap resource into the graph.

unique_filter_output.description: Output connector

$outputsize.description: The Output size parameter defines the horizontal and vertical size of the output image as a power of 2. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$format.description: The Output format parameter sets the output image format. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$pixelsize.description: The Pixel size parameter defines the horizontal and vertical size of a pixel. This parameter is used by the non-local filters: Blur, Sharpen, Transformation 2D, Directional blur, and Directional warp. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$pixelratio.description: The Pixel ratio parameter defines the behavior of the pixel size's aspect ratio with respect to the output size's aspect ratio. The parameter is used by non-local filters. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$tiling.description: The Tiling mode parameter defines the input’s tiling behavior. The parameter is used by the non-local filters. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$randomseed.description: The Random seed parameter defines the random seed of the filter node. This parameter is used by all random generators. Random seed is cumulative if the inherited qualifier is set. The final random seed is the sum of the random seeds of all of filters of its hierarchy. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

colorswitch.description: The Color mode parameter toggles between a grayscale and a color output image

bitmapresourcepath.description: The PKG resource path parameter displays the bitmap's location in your package

resizemethod.description: The Resize method parameter select how the bitmap is resized if required

blend.description: Combines two images using a specified blending mode and an optional mask.

opacity.description: Optional opacity input: per-pixel blending result mask

emboss.description: Applies an embossing effect by lighting the sides of shapes in an image according to a specified light source direction.

input1.description: Primary input image. Image to be processed

inputgradient.description: The Intensity input displaces the input 1 image’s color pixels in a given direction

unique_filter_output.description: Output connector

$outputsize.description: The Output size parameter defines the horizontal and vertical size of the output image as a power of 2. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$format.description: The Output format parameter sets the output image format. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$pixelsize.description: The Pixel size parameter defines the horizontal and vertical size of a pixel. This parameter is used by the non-local filters: Blur, Sharpen, Transformation 2D, Directional blur, and Directional warp. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$pixelratio.description: The Pixel ratio parameter defines the behavior of the pixel size's aspect ratio with respect to the output size's aspect ratio. The parameter is used by non-local filters. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$tiling.description: The Tiling mode parameter defines the input’s tiling behavior. The parameter is used by the non-local filters. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$randomseed.description: The Random seed parameter defines the random seed of the filter node. This parameter is used by all random generators. Random seed is cumulative if the inherited qualifier is set. The final random seed is the sum of the random seeds of all of filters of its hierarchy. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

intensity.description: The Intensity parameter adjusts the global intensity of illumination effect

lightangle.description: The Light angle defines the illumination angle of the embossed image's highlight

highlightcolor.description: The Highlight color parameter sets the color of the highlight if the input image is color

shadowcolor.description: The Shadow color parameter sets the color of the shadowed regions of the embossed image

fxmaps.description: Builds procedural noises and patterns by following the instructions of an FX-Map graph.

background.description: The Background parameter sets an optional color or grayscale input background image

unique_filter_output.description: Output connector

$outputsize.description: The Output size parameter defines the horizontal and vertical size of the output image as a power of 2. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$format.description: The Output format parameter sets the output image format. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$pixelsize.description: The Pixel size parameter defines the horizontal and vertical size of a pixel. This parameter is used by the non-local filters: Blur, Sharpen, Transformation 2D, Directional blur, and Directional warp. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$pixelratio.description: The Pixel ratio parameter defines the behavior of the pixel size's aspect ratio with respect to the output size's aspect ratio. The parameter is used by non-local filters. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$tiling.description: The Tiling mode parameter defines the input’s tiling behavior. The parameter is used by the non-local filters. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$randomseed.description: The Random seed parameter defines the random seed of the filter node. This parameter is used by all random generators. Random seed is cumulative if the inherited qualifier is set. The final random seed is the sum of the random seeds of all of filters of its hierarchy. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

colorswitch.description: The Color mode parameter toggles between a grayscale and a color output image

renderroi.description: The Render region parameter sets the region to render (left, right, top, bottom)

tilingroi.description: The Tiling region parameter sets a region of the FX-Map to tile (left, right, top, bottom)

culling.description: Activate Cull outside parameter in order to discard all patterns w/ centers outside the tiling region

hurst.description: The Roughness parameter calculates the roughness value (depth and opacity multiplier). It applies a bias to the FX-Map's blending process

opacitymult.description: The Global opacity parameter is an opacity multiplier applied to each FX-Map's patterns

gradient.description: Remaps the grayscale values in an image using a custom gradient.

input1.description: Primary input image. Image to be processed

unique_filter_output.description: Output connector

$format.description: The Output format parameter sets the output image format. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

colorswitch.description: The Color mode parameter toggles between the grayscale and the color image

addressingrepeat.description: This parameter determines how out of 0..1 range HDR pixels of the grayscale input are handled: clamped or fold up to 0..1.

grayscaleconversion.description: Converts a color image to grayscale by weighing the luminance of each color channel.

input1.description: Primary input image. Image to be processed

channelsweights.description: The Channels weights parameter defines the weightings for each channel. These weightings are used to calculate the grayscale result

alphamult.description: The Flatten alpha parameter multiplies the grayscale conversion by the input image’s alpha channel

mattelevel.description: The Background value parameter determines which pixels are to be treated as transparent. (Only used if the Flatten alpha parameter is set to true)

hsl.description: Adjusts the hue, saturation and lightness of a color image.

hue.description: The Hue parameter determines the color of the input image

saturation.description: The Saturation parameter determines the saturation of the input image's color

luminosity.description: The Lightness parameter determines the lightness of the input image

levelinlow.description: The Level in low parameter defines the input image's lowlight levels

levelinhigh.description: The Level in high parameter defines the input image's highlight levels

levelinmid.description: The Level in mid parameter defines the input image's midtone levels

leveloutlow.description: The Level out low parameter defines the output image's lowlight levels

levelouthigh.description: The Level out high parameter defines the output image's highlight levels

clampinterm.description: This parameter determines if transformed input value is clamped to 0..1 before computing output level

normal.description: Computes a normal map from a grayscale image interpreted as a height map.

input1.description: Input image grayscale; interpreted as a height map

passthrough.description:
The Dot node lets you reroute connections between nodes. Rerouted connections can be hidden by using Dot nodes as portals.

Note: this node is a layout tool only and has no impact on the result of the graph.

$tiling.description: The Tiling mode parameter defines the input’s tiling behavior. The parameter is used by the non-local filters. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$randomseed.description: The Random seed parameter defines the random seed of the filter node. This parameter is used by all random generators. Random seed is cumulative if the inherited qualifier is set. The final random seed is the sum of the random seeds of all of filters of its hierarchy. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

function.description: Function evaluated by this Value processor

warp.description: Displaces the pixel values in the input image according to the slopes computed from a separate gradient input, resulting in deformation.

input1.description: Primary input image. The color or grayscale image

inputgradient.description: The slope of the gradient of the grayscale input image determines the warp effect in the output image

unique_filter_output.description: Output connector

$outputsize.description: The Output size parameter defines the horizontal and vertical size of the output image as a power of 2. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$format.description: The Output format parameter sets the output image format. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$pixelsize.description: The Pixel size parameter defines the horizontal and vertical size of a pixel. This parameter is used by the non-local filters: Blur, Sharpen, Transformation 2D, Directional blur, and Directional warp. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$pixelratio.description: The Pixel ratio parameter defines the behavior of the pixel size's aspect ratio with respect to the output size's aspect ratio. The parameter is used by non-local filters. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$tiling.description: The Tiling mode parameter defines the input’s tiling behavior. The parameter is used by the non-local filters. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

$randomseed.description: The Random seed parameter defines the random seed of the filter node. This parameter is used by all random generators. Random seed is cumulative if the inherited qualifier is set. The final random seed is the sum of the random seeds of all of filters of its hierarchy. Parameter can be inherited from parent object, or set to an absolute value

intensity.description: The Intensity parameter sets the intensity of the warp

warpfilter.description: Controls whether bilinear or nearest filtering is used to sample the input
